name: cosmopolitan_identity
description: Measures the degree to which respondents identify as global citizens rather than exclusively as nationals, capturing openness to a supranational self-concept versus a Mexico-first orientation.

name: transnational_exposure
description: Measures the extent of respondents' direct personal experience with the world beyond Mexico, including international travel, residence abroad, language skills, and close ties to people living in other countries.

name: cultural_openness_to_foreign_influence
description: Measures receptivity to foreign cultural content and practices in daily life, including consumption of foreign media, music, food, and attitudes toward the diffusion of foreign ideas and customs in Mexico.

name: economic_globalization_attitudes
description: Measures respondents' evaluations of economic integration with the world, including opinions on free trade agreements, foreign investment, and the US-Mexico trade relationship.

name: international_institutional_engagement
description: Measures the degree to which respondents support Mexico's participation in and deference to international institutions and multilateral governance, including the UN, NGOs, and international human rights bodies.
